label: subsample PET (2M-train reweight; CV ~9% from GBDT) -- indicative only
known_issues_ref: KNOWN_ISSUES #12 (RESOLVED 2026-06-12): published 18.31% C_syst was garbage-miss-row inflated; rebank clean = 8.24%.
verdict: VERDICT: PET is COMPARABLE to GBDT in per-bin uncertainty on the 4796 common 4D bins (every PET bin is a GBDT bin; GBDT reports 34 extra). Median per-bin fractional uncertainty is 11.8% for PET (corrected: #12-clean rebank syst+stat+ML plus the PET-native lateral block) vs 13.4% for GBDT; the median per-bin ratio is 0.950 and PET is tighter in 54% of bins. So PET essentially MATCHES the GBDT precision rather than reducing it -- the point cloud agrees with the scalars on the central value AND lands at comparable uncertainty. Caveat 1 (covariance provenance): the raw published _wlat budget reads 22.5% because its C_syst was inflated by the KNOWN_ISSUES #12 garbage-miss-row bug; we headline the corrected rebank budget per the repo's adopted guidance. The lateral block is PET-NATIVE (independence-fair); the GBDT-transferred-lateral variant gives 23.0%, so the conclusion is not an artifact of the lateral treatment. Caveat 2 (stats regime): the PET covariance is anchored to the 2M-train reweight (pet_weights_full.npz), which still carries the PET-vs-GBDT CV training gap; the full-statistics PET (cal20m, job 55047632) was PENDING at run time, so this is INDICATIVE of the method, not a final full-stats uncertainty.
